qa_pairs.question: What is the initial action a Synchronization client performs when connected?
qa_pairs.answer: When you connect with a Synchronization client, it first synchronizes your files stored in the Alliance Nextcloud system with a copy of those files on your own computer, downloading all differing files to your client.

qa_pairs.question: How does a Synchronization client handle changes made to files?
qa_pairs.answer: When files are changed, they are re-copied to all synchronized systems to ensure consistency across all copies.

qa_pairs.question: What is a potential downside of using a Synchronization client when files are frequently updated?
qa_pairs.answer: The synchronization process can take a considerable amount of time if you or your collaborators change files frequently.

qa_pairs.question: What is a key benefit of using a Synchronization client?
qa_pairs.answer: A significant advantage is the ability to work on files offline, even without network connectivity.

qa_pairs.question: When are files synchronized if changes are made while offline using a Synchronization client?
qa_pairs.answer: Files will be synchronized when network connectivity is re-established.
